act_breakdown.act1: Effectively established within the desert location — introduces Miriam's character, the permit tension, and the thematic stakes of the final shot; compressed but efficient
act_breakdown.act2: Represented through the wrap sequence and van ride — emotional resolution between characters, philosophical exchange with Derek, and Miriam's quiet reflection serve as the emotional middle ground
act_breakdown.act3: Incomplete — the screenplay excerpt ends on an unresolved beat (the father's phone call), suggesting a larger personal arc that remains entirely undeveloped in this draft segment

recommendations: Expand the father phone call thread — it is the most dramatically promising unresolved element and needs full development to give Miriam a meaningful personal arc beyond professional competence

Deepen the Act 2 conflict; the permit tension is resolved too quickly and with too little consequence — raising the professional or interpersonal stakes would strengthen the middle section significantly

Consider broadening commercial appeal by grounding the philosophical dialogue in more visceral or emotionally accessible stakes, as the current tone risks alienating wider audiences despite strong character writing
